description_long: This is a two-story brick house with a flat concrete roof and visible signs of age and weathering, including patches of green moss and climbing vines on the exterior walls. The brickwork is uniform and red, with a horizontal concrete band separating the two stories. There are small, rectangular windows with dark frames on each level, and the windows are irregularly spaced, giving the facade an asymmetrical appearance. The main entryway features a simple green door located on the ground floor, with no porch or overhang, and is surrounded by more climbing vegetation. The overall structure is boxy and compact, with a utilitarian design typical of older urban residential buildings.
description: A weathered, two-story red brick house with a flat concrete roof, small windows, and climbing green vines. The facade is simple and boxy, with a green door as the main entry.
description_view_0: The side view shows two small windows, one on each story, with extensive green vines and moss covering much of the brick wall.
description_view_1: This view highlights the main entryway with a green door at the bottom and a single window above, with vines climbing up the wall.
description_view_2: The opposite side features a single window on the upper story and less vine coverage, with the brickwork and concrete band clearly visible.
description_view_3: This narrow side has no visible windows or doors, but shows a large patch of green moss and vines on the brick surface.
attribute_entryway_design: Simple green door at ground level, no porch or overhang, surrounded by climbing vines
